stories.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
stories.simple_summary: A tech company leader is discussing whether AI models (the thinking engines) should be separate from AI agents (the systems that actually do tasks). This is mostly a technical business debate.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
stories.simple_summary: Apple is making Siri (their voice assistant) sound more natural and less robotic by letting you adjust how fast she talks and how expressive she sounds.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use an iPhone, Siri might sound more like a real person soon, which could make it more pleasant to use.

stories.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
stories.simple_summary: Many big tech companies have fired workers this year and blamed AI as one of the reasons.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows companies are concerned AI might replace some jobs, which is worth being aware of if you work in tech or related fields.

stories.title: If you use Google, you’re training its AI. Here’s how to opt out.
stories.simple_summary: Google changed its settings so it can use more of your data to train its AI, but you can turn this off if you want.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Google and care about privacy, you should check your settings to decide whether you want Google using your information this way.

stories.title: I spy
stories.simple_summary: A TV show about smart glasses showed both the potential and the problems with this technology.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly for tech enthusiasts interested in glasses you can wear — not relevant to most people right now.

stories.title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft fired about 4,800 workers, mostly in their gaming division and sales team, saying AI changes played a role.
stories.why_it_matters: Like other layoffs, this shows large companies are shifting how they operate because of AI, but it doesn't directly affect you unless you work at Microsoft.

stories.title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
stories.simple_summary: Reddit is using AI to fight spam and fake posts on its site, because AI also helped create the problem in the first place.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Reddit, the site may have cleaner discussions in the future as they fight spam more effectively.

stories.title: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees, about 2% of its total workforce, mostly from sales and the Xbox gaming business.
stories.why_it_matters: This confirms large tech companies are reorganizing, but it directly affects you only if you work there or depend on Xbox services.

stories.title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
stories.simple_summary: A startup hub in Paris is helping European AI companies grow and find funding.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people starting AI companies — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
stories.simple_summary: Some wealthy families are using AI programs instead of traditional schools to teach their children.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're thinking about education options, know that some families are experimenting with AI tutors, though this is mostly available to the very wealthy right now.

stories.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is stopping its Mechanical Turk program, which let people earn money by doing small online tasks.
stories.why_it_matters: If you used Mechanical Turk to make extra money, you won't be able to do this through Amazon anymore after the changeover.

stories.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
stories.simple_summary: Google made a commercial showing the Founding Fathers using Google's AI and collaboration tools, and many people thought it was embarrassing.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just advertising news — it doesn't affect how Google products actually work.
